reasoning: The market value currently sits at 3.45%, which strongly suggests that the crowd believes it is highly unlikely Elon Musk will step down as Tesla CEO by the end of 2025. He recently had his massive compensation package re-approved by shareholders, tying him closer to the company, and despite his many other ventures (SpaceX, xAI, X, DOGE), he remains firmly entrenched as Tesla's CEO with apparent strong support from the board. Barring an unforeseen health crisis, sudden resignation to focus entirely on government/other ventures, or an extreme scandal, he is expected to remain in his position. Accordingly, a baseline probability reflecting standard continuity and low expected turnover roughly matches the market consensus.
